login.heading: Sign in to Tenio
login.error: Invalid credentials. Try again or contact support.

retrieve.label: Request status check
retrieve.tooltip: Fetch the latest status from the payer's system. This doesn't change any official claim state.
retrieve.loading: Fetching payer snapshot... This usually takes 1–3 minutes.
retrieve.completed: Payer snapshot updated. Check the Evidence tab for results.

queue.heading: Your follow-up workday
queue.subheading: Claims that need your attention today, sorted by urgency. Work top to bottom.
queue.filterPlaceholder: Filter by payer, status, or assigned owner
queue.searchAllClaimsCta: Looking for a specific claim? Search all claims →

roleHelp.manager: View all claims in the queue. Reassign ownership. Request status checks.
roleHelp.operator: Work the queue. Open claims, log follow-ups, and request status checks.
roleHelp.viewer: View queue items and claim status. You cannot take actions from this page.

empty.noAttentionBody: All claims are up to date or waiting on payer responses. Check back when new claims are imported or SLA timers approach.
empty.noClaimsTitle: Your queue is waiting for claims
empty.noClaimsBody: Import your active claims to start working follow-ups. Once imported, claims that need attention will appear here.
empty.noMatchesBody: Try adjusting your search or filters to see more queue items.

claims.heading: All claims
claims.subheading: Your full claim inventory. Search, sort, and open any claim to see its history.
claims.searchPlaceholder: Search by patient name, claim number, or payer
claims.exportTooltip: Download a CSV of your current filtered claim list.
claims.intakeBody: Create one claim manually and place it into the follow-up workflow.
claims.goToQueueCta: Ready to start working? Go to your queue →

roleHelp.manager: View all claims and export the current list.
roleHelp.operator: View all claims and add a single claim manually.

empty.title: No claims yet
empty.body: Import your active claim inventory to get started. Tenio supports Jane App CSV exports and other common formats.

noSearch.title: No claims match your search
noSearch.body: Try adjusting your filters or search term. If you're looking for a resolved claim, check the Resolved tab.

onboarding.title: Import claims
onboarding.subtitle: Export your active claims from Jane App and upload the file below. Tenio will match the columns automatically and show you a preview before anything is saved.
onboarding.importInstructionsBody: Drag and drop your CSV file here, or click to browse. Supported format: Jane App CSV export from the Billing tab.
onboarding.commitButtonSubtext: Only valid rows will be imported.
onboarding.configuredPayersBody: These are the payer profiles currently available for imported claims.
onboarding.previewResultsBody: Tenio will only import rows marked as new claims or updates.
onboarding.templateProfileHelp: Choose the profile that best matches your file before previewing it.
onboarding.profileJaneDescription: Best fit for Ottawa paramedical clinics exporting active claims from Jane App.
onboarding.profileGenericDescription: Use this when your file already matches the Tenio template columns.
onboarding.previewGeneratedNotice: Preview ready. Review each row before importing.
onboarding.uploadRequiredError: Upload a CSV file before previewing the import.
onboarding.previewRequiredError: Preview the file first. Only valid rows can be imported.
onboarding.previewFailedError: We couldn't preview that file. Check the format and try again.
onboarding.commitFailedError: We couldn't import those claims. No changes were saved.

roleHelp.viewer: Import is not available for your role.

firstVisit.title: Ready to import your first claims?
firstVisit.body: Export your active claims from Jane App and upload the file below. Tenio will walk you through a preview before anything is saved.
firstVisit.helper: Not sure how to export from Jane App? Go to Billing → Claims → Export as CSV.

onboarding.noAccessBody: Import is not available for your role.

setup.title: Workspace setup
setup.subtitle: Complete these steps to get your team working in Tenio.
setup.skipLockedMessage: Complete your first import before skipping this setup guide.
setup.updateError: The setup checklist could not be updated. Try again.
setup.welcomeTitle: Welcome to Tenio. Let's get your workspace ready.
setup.welcomeBody: Add your team, import your claims, configure your payers, and review your queue before you start working live follow-ups.

team_members.title: Add your team
team_members.description: Contact support to add operators and managers to your account.

first_import.title: Import your first claims
first_import.description: Upload a CSV export from your practice management software.

configure_payers.title: Configure your payers
configure_payers.description: Set follow-up rules for each payer your practice works with.

review_first_queue.title: Review your first queue
review_first_queue.description: Open the queue, inspect priorities, and optionally open a claim to see how follow-up works.

priority.title: Priority and SLA risk
priority.body: Red means a claim is overdue. Orange means it's at risk. Start with red — those need attention today.

claim.title: Open a claim from the claim number
claim.body: Use the claim number to open a claim, review its history, and log what happened.

sla.title: Watch the Age / SLA timer
sla.body: This timer shows how long since the claim was last touched. Your goal: keep every claim moving forward.

claim.pageSubheading: Follow one claim from import through verified resolution.

sections.agentInterpretationBody: Tenio's latest interpretation of this claim and the next recommended step.
sections.evidenceBody: Payer snapshots and supporting artifacts captured for this claim.

agent.whyRouted: Why it was routed this way

actions.readOnly: This role can review the claim but cannot change workflow state.

evidence.emptyBody: Request a status check to fetch the latest payer snapshot. Evidence will appear here once a retrieval completes.

timeline.emptyBody: Every action taken on this claim will appear here — status checks, follow-up notes, and workflow changes.

claim.readOnly: This role can review the claim but cannot change workflow state.
claim.downloadRestricted: Evidence download is limited to owner, manager, and operator roles.

followUp.limitedAccess: Structured follow-up is limited to owner, manager, and operator roles.
followUp.reviewNotePlaceholder: Add context, rationale, or a workflow note.
followUp.structuredBody: Log what happened and what comes next.
followUp.notePlaceholder: Document what happened and what comes next.

results.heading: Resolved claims
results.subheading: Claims that have reached a verified outcome. Export for reconciliation or audit.
results.pageNote: Verified outcomes. These claims have been through the full follow-up cycle and are ready for reconciliation or audit.
results.searchPlaceholder: Search by result ID, claim ID, or payer

roleHelp.manager: View resolved claims and export results.

empty.title: No resolved claims yet
empty.body: Claims will appear here once they reach a verified outcome. Keep working the queue — resolved claims land here automatically.

resultDetail.subtitle: Verified outcome snapshot ready for reconciliation or audit.
resultDetail.verifiedBody: This claim has reached a verified outcome and is ready for reconciliation or audit.

performance.heading: Performance
performance.subheading: How your team and automation are performing across your claim inventory.

sections.resolutionSnapshotBody: Claims worked per day and unresolved backlog over time.
sections.connectorHealthBody: Runtime reliability, retries, and failures by connector.

performance.noConnectorActivity: No connector activity has been recorded yet.

configuration.heading: Payer configuration
configuration.subheadingOwner: Set follow-up rules for each payer. Changes take effect on new queue items.
configuration.subheadingManager: Current payer follow-up rules. Contact your owner to make changes.
configuration.sidebarBody: Live payer configuration and rule ownership.

roleHelp.manager: View payer policies. Contact your owner to make changes.
roleHelp.viewer: Configuration is not available for your role.

configuration.workflowPolicyBody: Set follow-up rules for each payer your practice works with.

fields.defaultOwnerHelp: Who owns new claims from this payer by default
fields.reviewThresholdHelp: Flag for review when confidence drops below this %
fields.escalationThresholdHelp: Escalate when confidence drops below this %
fields.defaultSlaHelp: Target resolution window for claims from this payer
fields.autoAssignHelp: Automatically assign claims to the default owner

configuration.savedMessage: Policy saved. New thresholds will apply to future intake and retrieval runs.
configuration.readOnlyHelp: Only owners can save payer policy changes.

sections.auditTrailBody: Recent configuration changes from the audit log.

auditLog.heading: Audit log
auditLog.subheading: A complete record of every action taken in your workspace — by people and by the system.
auditLog.searchPlaceholder: Search claim ID, actor, note, or action

common.noEvents: No audit events are available for the pilot yet.

status.heading: Workspace status
status.subheading: A quick health check on your Tenio workspace.

cardBodies.claimsImported: Rows processed in the latest import commit.
cardBodies.lastQueueSync: Latest queue update across active claims.
cardBodies.failedActions: Audit events recorded with a failure outcome.
cardBodies.openClaims: Claims not yet resolved in the current workspace.

errors.queueUnavailableBody: The queue could not load from the API. Confirm the API and Postgres are running, then refresh the page.
errors.claimsUnavailableBody: The claims workspace could not load from the API. Confirm the API and database are healthy, then refresh.
errors.onboardingUnavailableBody: The onboarding workspace could not load import and payer setup data. Confirm the API is healthy, then refresh.
errors.configurationUnavailableBody: The payer configuration workspace could not load live configuration data. Confirm the API is healthy, then refresh.
errors.performanceUnavailableBody: The performance dashboard could not load live metrics from the API. Confirm the API is healthy, then refresh.
errors.auditUnavailableBody: The audit log could not load from the API. Confirm the API and Postgres container are healthy, then refresh.
errors.statusLoadBody: The status view could not load from the API. Confirm the API and database are healthy, then refresh.
errors.statusForbiddenBody: Only owner and manager roles can view workspace status.
errors.resultsUnavailableBody: The resolved claims view could not load from the API. Confirm the API and Postgres container are healthy, then refresh.
errors.claimDetailUnavailableBody: The claim detail view for claim {claimId} could not load from the API. Confirm the API and database are healthy, then try again.
errors.resultDetailUnavailableBody: The result detail view for result {resultId} could not load from the API. Confirm the API and database are healthy, then try again.
